question: What is the empty working area in Grasshopper where you place and connect components called?
options: The Canvas
The Toolbar
The Viewport
The Panel

question: How do you quickly search for a component by name directly on the canvas?
options: Open the Rhino File menu
Double-click an empty area of the canvas and type its name
Press Ctrl+S
Right-click the Rhino viewport

question: A component's wire turns red on the canvas. What does this usually indicate?
options: The component is currently selected
The component is disabled
The component produced an error
The data is a tree

question: Which component would you use to simply add two numbers together?
options: Merge
Series
List Item
Addition

question: What is the key difference between a Number Slider and a Panel?
options: Both components only generate random values
Both components are used only for typing text
A Slider only displays data, while a Panel generates it
A Slider generates/outputs a numeric value, while a Panel only displays incoming data

question: What is a 'parameter' component (e.g. Number, Point, Curve) used for in Grasshopper?
options: To store and hold a specific type of data for use by other components
To save the Grasshopper file to disk
To render the final 3D view of the scene
To perform a mathematical operation on incoming data

question: How can you type an exact numeric value into a Number Slider instead of dragging it?
options: Double-click the slider and type the value directly
Press Ctrl+Z while hovering over the slider
Drag the slider completely off the canvas
Right-click the slider and choose 'Delete'

question: What can you configure by right-clicking a Number Slider?
options: The color of every wire on the canvas
The rotation of the Rhino viewport
Its minimum, maximum, and number of decimal places
The export settings for the whole file

question: What does a Panel component do by default?
options: Generates a new random slider value
Executes custom Python code
Deletes duplicate wires on the canvas
Displays the data connected to its input as text

question: How do you connect two components together using the mouse?
options: Drag one component completely on top of the other
Click and drag from one component's output point to another's input point (or vice versa)
Right-click the canvas and choose 'Auto Connect'
Double-click both components at the same time

question: How do you remove a single wire connection using only the mouse?
options: Double-click on either connected component
Right-click the canvas background and choose 'Clear All'
Click and drag the wire's end away from the input, then release it over empty canvas space
Press Ctrl+A and then Delete

question: Besides dragging it away, what is another mouse-based way to delete a wire?
options: Scroll the mouse wheel while hovering over the wire
Click directly on the wire to select it, then press the Delete key
Hover the mouse over the wire without clicking
Middle-click twice on the wire

question: If an input already has a wire connected and you drag a new wire onto that same single-item input, what typically happens?
options: The new connection replaces the previous one
Nothing happens; the new wire is ignored
Grasshopper closes without saving
Both wires stay connected and their values are averaged automatically

question: Which component retrieves a single item from a list at a specified index?
options: Cull Pattern
List Length
List Item
Sort List

question: What does the 'Cull Pattern' component do to a list?
options: Removes items from a list based on a repeating true/false pattern
Reverses the order of a list
Combines multiple lists into one
Sorts a list numerically

question: Which component joins multiple lists end-to-end into a single combined list?
options: Dispatch
List Item
Split List
Merge

question: What does the 'Dispatch' component do?
options: Removes duplicate values from a list
Reverses the order of a list
Splits a list into two separate lists (A/B) based on a boolean pattern
Sorts items alphabetically

question: If you want to know how many items are in a list, which component do you use?
options: Series
Range
List Item
List Length

question: Which component splits a single list into two lists at a given index?
options: Graft
Flatten Tree
Split List
Merge

question: What is a 'branch' in a Grasshopper data tree?
options: A wire connecting two components
A collection of grouped components
A single numeric value within a list
A sub-list of data identified by a unique path, e.g. {0;0}

question: In a data tree path such as {0;1;2}, what do the numbers represent?
options: The X, Y, Z coordinates of a point
The nested branch indices that locate that specific branch within the tree
The color values of the wire
The number of items stored in that branch

question: Which component or action flattens an entire data tree into a single list (removing all branch structure)?
options: Path Mapper
Graft Tree
Flatten (via the Flatten Tree component, or right-click 'Flatten' on a parameter)
Series

question: What does 'Graft' do to a list of data?
options: Sorts branches numerically by path
Puts each individual item into its own separate branch
Combines all branches into a single flat list
Deletes all empty branches

question: Besides using dedicated components, how else can you quickly apply Graft or Flatten to a wire's data in Grasshopper?
options: Only by writing a C# script
Only by installing a third-party plugin
It cannot be done without rebuilding the whole definition
Right-click the input or output parameter and choose 'Graft' or 'Flatten' from the menu

question: You feed two separate lists of 5 items each into a component like 'Move' or 'Line', but you graft one of the lists first. What is the typical effect?
options: Each item is now on its own branch, so the component pairs every item of one list with every item of the other (a cross-product), instead of matching them one-to-one
The data is permanently deleted
All the numbers are converted into text
Grafting has no effect on how components combine data

question: Which component extracts a specific branch from a data tree using its path?
options: Tree Branch
Merge
Simplify Tree
Flatten Tree

question: What does the 'Simplify Tree' component do?
options: Deletes only the empty branches in a tree
Combines two separate trees into one
Converts a tree directly into a flat list
Removes redundant path segments that are common to every branch

question: What is the fundamental building block of a mesh's geometry?
options: A single continuous mathematical surface equation
Voxels
Control points and knot vectors
Vertices, edges, and planar faces (typically triangles or quads)

question: What defines the shape of a NURBS surface internally?
options: A fixed grid of triangles
Control points, weights, and knot vectors
A fixed grid of pixels
Vertices and faces only

question: Which of these is a real advantage of meshes over NURBS?
options: NURBS are always lighter in memory than meshes
Meshes are always mathematically exact and perfectly smooth
Meshes cannot represent curved-looking surfaces at all
Meshes handle complex, irregular topology efficiently and are fast to render/compute

question: Which of these is a real advantage of NURBS over meshes?
options: NURBS surfaces cannot be trimmed
NURBS always compute faster in real-time display
NURBS are always better for very high-poly organic sculpting
NURBS surfaces are mathematically exact and produce smooth, precise curved geometry

question: Which Grasshopper component converts a NURBS Brep/Surface into a Mesh?
options: Boundary Surfaces
Deconstruct Brep
Brep Edges
Mesh Brep

question: What happens when you convert a Mesh into a Brep in Grasshopper (e.g. via 'Mesh to Brep')?
options: The mesh is converted into a point cloud
It is not possible to convert a mesh into a Brep
Each planar mesh face becomes its own individual flat NURBS surface; no smooth curvature is added
The mesh automatically becomes one smooth continuous NURBS surface

question: What is a Grasshopper 'plugin'?
options: A built-in Rhino rendering mode
An add-on library that adds extra components or functionality beyond the core install
A special type of data tree
The file format used to save Grasshopper definitions

question: Which two scripting languages have dedicated built-in components in standard Grasshopper for writing custom code?
options: JavaScript and Ruby
C# and Python
MATLAB and R
Java and C++

question: What is a common way to install a new plugin (.gha file) so Grasshopper can load it?
options: Place it in the Windows System32 folder
Drag it directly into the Rhino viewport
Rename the file to .exe and run it
Place it in the Grasshopper Libraries folder, or install it via Rhino's Package Manager

question: Why might a designer install a third-party plugin instead of only using native Grasshopper components?
options: To gain specialized functionality (e.g. simulation, analysis, or fabrication tools) without building it from scratch
To automatically convert Grasshopper files into images
To permanently disable the native components
To make the Grasshopper canvas load faster

question: What does the C# Script component let you do that is hard to achieve with standard components alone?
options: Only display panels of text
Write custom logic and algorithms directly with full programming control
Automatically create new number sliders
Import Rhino files without opening Rhino

question: Before installing a new plugin, what should you generally check first?
options: That it was released within the last week
That it only works with one specific scripting language
That its icon is a specific color
That it is compatible with your installed Rhino/Grasshopper version
